sub_instance.answer_contract: Object with "factory_a", "factory_b", and "makespan". Each factory maps machine names to lists of ["J<id>", start, end] triples.
sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 5 jobs, 6 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA1 (7) -> MA5 (2) -> MA2 (5) -> MA4 (4) -> MA3 (6) -> MA6 (6); Factory B MB6 (5) -> MB1 (6) -> MB4 (9) -> MB2 (3) -> MB3 (3) -> MB5 (9)
- Job 2: Factory A MA6 (7) -> MA3 (7) -> MA2 (8) -> MA1 (2) -> MA5 (2) -> MA4 (6); Factory B MB5 (3) -> MB3 (2) -> MB1 (8) -> MB4 (9) -> MB2 (4) -> MB6 (5)
- Job 3: Factory A MA4 (1) -> MA5 (3) -> MA2 (7) -> MA6 (2) -> MA3 (9) -> MA1 (2); Factory B MB1 (7) -> MB6 (8) -> MB5 (3) -> MB2 (9) -> MB3 (7) -> MB4 (8)
- Job 4: Factory A MA3 (2) -> MA4 (3) -> MA5 (8) -> MA2 (5) -> MA6 (8) -> MA1 (1); Factory B MB3 (7) -> MB4 (7) -> MB5 (1) -> MB1 (6) -> MB2 (6) -> MB6 (8)
- Job 5: Factory A MA3 (2) -> MA6 (1) -> MA5 (4) -> MA4 (5) -> MA1 (1) -> MA2 (1); Factory B MB6 (9) -> MB5 (5) -> MB4 (9) -> MB2 (6) -> MB1 (5) -> MB3 (2)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.

Factory B: MB1: J1[35-41],J2[70-78],J3[86-93],J4[139-145],J5[188-193]; MB2: J1[50-53],J2[87-91],J3[107-116],J4[145-151],J5[182-188]; MB3: J1[53-56],J2[68-70],J3[116-123],J4[123-130],J5[193-195]; MB4: J1[41-50],J2[78-87],J3[123-131],J4[131-138],J5[173-182]; MB5: J1[56-65],J2[65-68],J3[104-107],J4[138-139],J5[168-173]; MB6: J1[30-35],J2[91-96],J3[96-104],J4[151-159],J5[159-168]
Makespan: 195

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 5 jobs, 6 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA1 (3) -> MA2 (4) -> MA5 (4) -> MA6 (1) -> MA3 (7) -> MA4 (4); Factory B MB4 (2) -> MB2 (2) -> MB5 (7) -> MB1 (6) -> MB6 (3) -> MB3 (3)
- Job 2: Factory A MA5 (9) -> MA6 (9) -> MA3 (5) -> MA4 (2) -> MA1 (7) -> MA2 (3); Factory B MB2 (4) -> MB6 (6) -> MB1 (2) -> MB5 (1) -> MB3 (7) -> MB4 (8)
- Job 3: Factory A MA3 (1) -> MA2 (4) -> MA4 (4) -> MA5 (1) -> MA6 (6) -> MA1 (6); Factory B MB2 (7) -> MB6 (2) -> MB3 (8) -> MB4 (3) -> MB1 (8) -> MB5 (9)
- Job 4: Factory A MA2 (4) -> MA5 (4) -> MA4 (1) -> MA3 (1) -> MA6 (7) -> MA1 (7); Factory B MB6 (9) -> MB4 (5) -> MB2 (8) -> MB5 (3) -> MB3 (9) -> MB1 (9)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (2) -> MA4 (5) -> MA3 (9) -> MA5 (8) -> MA2 (2) -> MA6 (3); Factory B MB2 (4) -> MB4 (3) -> MB6 (9) -> MB3 (2) -> MB1 (8) -> MB5 (9)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.
